{"id":"project-LICHEN-3cwf","title":"Direct message dest_iid can be all zeros when None - semantically invalid","description":"File: python/src/lichen/gateway/compact_cot.py
Lines: 635-639

When encoding a DIRECT chat message:
```python
elif chat.dest_type == DestType.DIRECT:
    iid = chat.dest_iid or bytes(8)
    if len(iid) != 8:
        raise ValueError('dest_iid must be exactly 8 bytes')
    parts.append(iid)
```

If chat.dest_iid is None, this silently substitutes 8 zero bytes as the destination IID.

Problems:
1. An all-zeros IID (0x0000000000000000) is likely not a valid node identifier
2. Message would be routed to the wrong recipient or nowhere
3. Masks a programming error (None IID for DIRECT dest_type)

Fix: Raise an error for missing direct destination:
```python
elif chat.dest_type == DestType.DIRECT:
    if chat.dest_iid is None:
        raise ValueError('dest_iid required when dest_type is DIRECT')
    if len(chat.dest_iid) != 8:
        raise ValueError('dest_iid must be exactly 8 bytes')
    parts.append(chat.dest_iid)
```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:17:30.18619Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:17:30.186224Z"}
